expertise_area: Activism, Modeling, Toxic Shock Syndrome Awareness

key_insights.insight: There is no true forgiveness for companies who knowingly use harmful substances; the fight for equality and safe products is a constant endeavor.
key_insights.quote: There's just this unjust that sits within me to know that when I see a little girl walking down the street, I see myself... and not ever wanting that to ever happen to another soul.

key_insights.insight: Perseverance is key to finding purpose and making a difference even when the future looks bleak.
key_insights.quote: You are the light at the end of the tunnel. You know, you being here and doing what you're doing,you are that light.

key_insights.insight: The world is not built for accessibility and that people with disabilities are often looked down upon.
key_insights.quote: I learned that, it's just not... there's just like stigma of incapablebecause you maybe looka certain way or because you're confined in a certain space or the world is not built for that.

key_insights.insight: There is no rushing time or forcing a moment to happen; you must sit with your feelings, good or bad, in order to move forward.
key_insights.quote: Time, time, time, you can't rush it. You have no controlover it. And I think that's when it's those moments when you have to sit in it, sit with it,feel it, feel every part of it.

key_insights.insight: Even organic tampons still have pesticides, making them toxic. There is nothing on the market that is a safe feminine hygiene product.
key_insights.quote: You're using organic, but it's still sprayed with pesticides.You're still putting poison inside of you.

key_insights.insight: It is up to the individual to educate themselves and be aware that toxic shock syndrome can happen to anyone.
key_insights.quote: Just be more aware, educate yourself... not just thinking that it can't happen to you because it can happen to anyone at any time.

key_insights.insight: It is essential to create space for and to allow young children to ask questions and be curious about differences.
key_insights.quote: It should be like, you should ask the questions and you should wonder because that kid now is going to see someone like myself or just think that I'm the coolest thing on the planetinstead of leaving thinking that there's something wrong or that I'm incapable or unable to dosomething.

actionable_advice.advice: If struggling to find a purpose, look to see what's unfair, and see if you can help fix it.
actionable_advice.how_to: Look to your trauma and see if there is an injustice happening.

actionable_advice.advice: Examine the ingredients of feminine hygiene products.
actionable_advice.how_to: Read the labels on tampons and pads to see if they contain harmful chemicals and become more aware of the dangers

memorable_stories: Lauren's memory of her blind cocker spaniel ferociously barking at her to wake her up before the police arrived, highlighting the dog's life-saving instincts.
The story of a little boy in Switzerland wanting "golden legs" after seeing Lauren's prosthetics, illustrating a child's innocent acceptance and admiration of difference.
Lauren's godfather playing and singing Bob Marley's "Three Little Birds" to her every day while she was in the hospital.
Lauren's godfather's advice to amputate both legs to 'just move on with your life'.
The missed voicemail from her Godfather wishing to see her new house.
contrarian_claims: The path to strength is often vulnerability, talking, crying and asking for help.
That there is nothing on the feminine hygiene market that is safe.
That healthcare should provide options for life not just basic needs.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Shocking TRUE Story: “I Lost Both Of My Legs Because Of A Tampon” (Health Warning) - Lauren Wasser
